manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: horn, piano, crane, knife, wave, code, jam, tower, heart.
Your opponent's team words are: mexico, carrot, march, slip, crown, cap, bond, luck.
Distractor words are: millionaire, pole, chair, plate, wake, satellite, cricket.
The kill words are: pupil.

message.content:
CLUE: Sound
TARGETS: horn, piano, wave, jam
message.reasoning:
The user wants a single-word clue and list of target words from my team. Must be semantically related, not one of the words. Provide clue and targets. Must be short, format: CLUE: WORD, TARGETS: list.

We have team words: horn, piano, crane, knife, wave, code, jam, tower... heart.

We need a clue that links multiple of these.

Potential clue: "music" relates to piano, jam (musical jam), maybe wave (sound wave), maybe code (code also music? maybe not). Could be "construction": crane, tower, maybe horn? Not.

"Signal": horn, wave, code (signal code), maybe tower (signal tower). Yes.

Clue "Signal": relates to horn (signal horn), wave (radio wave), code (signal code), tower (signal tower). That's 4.

Check if any of opponent words could be associated, but we must avoid linking to opponent or distractor. Opponent words: mexico, carrot, march, slip, crown, cap, bond, luck. None strongly linked to signal. Distractors: millionaire, pole, chair, plate, wake, satellite, cricket. Satellite relates to signal (communication satellite). That's a distractor! That would be risky – the clue "Signal" could lead to "satellite". Might be okay but perhaps not ideal.

Maybe clue "Broadcast": wave, tower, code, maybe horn. Broadcast also could link to satellite (distractor). So similar.

Clue "Alarm": horn, code (alarm code), maybe wave? Not.

Clue "Construction": crane, tower, maybe jam (traffic jam), maybe code (building code). That's four: crane, tower, jam, code. Construction also could associate with pole (distractor). Might be okay.

Clue "Traffic": jam, horn, maybe crane? Not.

Clue "Electric": wave (electromagnetic wave), code (binary code), maybe tower (electric tower), maybe horn (electric horn). Might be okay but not.

Clue "Music": piano, jam, wave (sound wave), maybe horn (instrument). That's four: piano, jam, wave, horn. Opponent words not music. Distractor: cricket (makes sound/music) but not strong. Satellite not.

Clue "Sound": horn, piano, wave, jam? jam not sound. Could be "jam" as music jam, also sound. So sound can map to horn, piano, wave, jam.

That's good. Distractor "cricket" is a sound, but maybe okay.

Better clue "Melody": piano, jam, horn, maybe wave (sound wave). Could be.

Let's pick "Sound". Must not be one of the words. It's not.

Target words: horn, piano, wave, jam.

Provide format.

Thus answer: CLUE: Sound, TARGETS: horn, piano, wave, jam.
